npcs.name: Dr. Sera Valez
wants: Prevent the clinic becoming a battlefield
secrets: Knows Mara covertly supports local schools and food programs
npcs.gm_notes: If the PCs interact with her, she pushes negotiation, evacuation, and containment. She is not naive and will not hand civilians to corporate recovery.

npcs.name: Mara “Mother Red” Vey
wants: Get proof of what happened to missing locals
Prevent the corporation from using the clinic as a cleanup site
secrets: Mara covertly funds food, medicine, and school support
Mara knows Bex and Lala are missing and suspects this is not normal gang business
npcs.gm_notes: Mara becomes more cooperative if PCs prove Redline people are being taken by something in the service infrastructure.

npcs.name: Nox “Bluewire” Kade
npcs.role: Volatile Redline enforcer and cyber-strain casualty
npcs.attitude: Agitated, frightened, pain-ridden, desperate to be useful
wants: Protect Redline people from a threat he cannot explain
offers: Instinctive warning about clicking in the walls
Memory of missing people near service routes
secrets: Bluewire is not alien-controlled
His distress is opioids, cyber-strain, trauma, and possibly illegal treatment side effects
He heard or saw signs of Model 3 activity and was dismissed because he is unstable
npcs.gm_notes: Keep Bluewire human. He is an early warning system because nobody believes him when he says the walls have teeth.

npcs.name: Commander Ilan Rusk
npcs.attitude: Professional, hostile, rational, and wrong about the current threat
wants: Recover or destroy the Quill Relay
Prevent Redline Choir from broadcasting evidence
offers: Knowledge of the old nest site
secrets: Rusk knows the corporation created and destroyed the first nest
Rusk does not initially know the second hidden nest exists
His team’s mission includes witness control
npcs.gm_notes: Rusk can truthfully say the first nest was sterilized, while lying about experiments and cleanup orders. Once the second nest is proven, he must choose whether to cooperate or double down.

npcs.name: Keet
secrets: Saw the courier arrive and noticed the relay pulse before anyone touched it
npcs.gm_notes: Good NPC for pulling sympathetic PCs toward the school annex and civilian stakes.

npcs.name: Sister Luma
wants: Have people treat suffering as real
secrets: She has seen similar distress patterns before in patients exposed to forbidden tech
npcs.gm_notes: Useful for encouraging players to investigate psychology, fear, grief, and pain.

npcs.name: Tamsin Quill
npcs.role: Courier who stole the Quill Relay
wants: Keep the relay away from Corporate Recovery
Warn that the corporation is cleaning witnesses, not rescuing people
offers: Route from the old research annex
Fragmentary memory of Model 1s flying away during cleanup
secrets: Tamsin saw evidence that the first nest cleanup failed
The relay is evidence, not alien technology
npcs.gm_notes: Use Tamsin as the inciting body and warning. A clear line: “They weren’t fleeing. They were planting something.”

npcs.name: Mote Swarm
secrets: The swarm is a symptom, not the main alien intelligence
npcs.gm_notes: Use to force cooperation, evacuation, and first ascension. Do not make this a social faction in the starter job.

npcs.name: Florence “NightCrash” Vale
npcs.role: Level 5 Samurai / combat medic / evacuation specialist
wants: Welcome newly awakened Samurai if the PCs ascend
offers: One tactical opening during an alien breach
secrets: She is already being redirected to a larger emergency elsewhere
Her cheerful public persona hides severe fatigue
She will not solve the PCs' final decision for them
npcs.gm_notes: Use NightCrash only as an emergency safety valve or post-victory recognition cameo. She arrives briefly, saves wounded or creates one opening, then rushes to a bigger emergency. She is adverse to fighting humans and focuses on evacuation, medicine, and fighting aliens.

npcs.name: The Siren Saint
npcs.attitude: Vehicle asset, not a social NPC
secrets: The hoverbike itself does not carry the patient; the detachable trauma pod does
npcs.gm_notes: Use as explanation for how NightCrash arrives quickly without pretending a critically injured person fits on a normal hoverbike.

npcs.name: Gurney Angels
offers: Carry two small civilians in an emergency
secrets: They are sponsored human tech, not alien drones
npcs.gm_notes: The Gurney Angels are evacuation tools, not combat monsters. They can briefly block or shove through danger while carrying a patient, but their main job is extraction.

npcs.name: Juno “Switch” Hale
npcs.role: Redline camera sitter and unofficial surveillance operator
npcs.attitude: Sharp, defensive, overworked, more frightened than they admit
wants: Find out what happened to Bex Aranda and Lala Mir
offers: Footage of Bex being dragged toward a service gap
secrets: Switch has seen low, fast shapes on corrupted footage
Switch suspects the missing people are not ordinary gang/corp casualties
npcs.gm_notes: Use Switch as a social alternative to hacking. Cooperation can reveal that people disappeared near routes connected to the hidden nest.

npcs.name: Rafa Mbeki
wants: Avoid turning clinic camera feeds into corporate evidence against patients
secrets: Rafa noticed camera gaps but feared reporting them would trigger a corporate raid
npcs.gm_notes: Use Rafa as the clinic-side control room NPC. He can establish a pattern but not solve the whole mystery without physical investigation.

npcs.name: Oskar Venn
npcs.attitude: Absent; known by coworkers as careful and stubborn
wants: Before vanishing: fix the HVAC fault
offers: Radio log: “Something is breathing in here.”
secrets: Oskar was taken by a juvenile Model 3
His remains may be partly incorporated near the hidden nest
npcs.gm_notes: Use Oskar as the cleanest technical missing-person clue.

npcs.name: Laleh “Lala” Mir
npcs.attitude: Absent; remembered as warm, reliable, and brave
wants: Before vanishing: deliver food through back corridors
secrets: Lala was taken near B10
Her disappearance grounds the Auntie Red reveal
npcs.gm_notes: Use Lala to link Mara’s covert support and the missing-person threat.

npcs.name: Bex Aranda
npcs.attitude: Absent; officially accused of desertion by angry underlings
wants: Before vanishing: watch the service approach
offers: Drag marks up into a service gap
A dropped Redline token or knife
secrets: Bex was taken by a juvenile Model 3
Mara suspects more than she admits
npcs.gm_notes: Use Bex as the Redline emotional hook.

npcs.name: Narin Pell
wants: Before vanishing: stay hidden and avoid authorities
offers: Possible rescue if PCs act quickly
secrets: Narin may still be alive early in the session
If PCs delay, Narin is incorporated into the nest
npcs.gm_notes: Use Narin as the possible rescue victim.

npcs.name: Halden Rook
npcs.attitude: Dead or missing; cautious, technically competent, ignored
wants: Before vanishing: warn that Model 1 flight was not random
offers: Final note: “They’re not fleeing. They’re relocating.”
secrets: Halden’s warning was buried
Corporate Recovery may know the name but not volunteer it
npcs.gm_notes: Use Halden as the deep relay clue.

npcs.name: Juvenile Model 3
wants: Drag prey back to the hidden nest
offers: Proof that a new nest exists
A rescue opportunity if it is dragging a victim
secrets: Only a few exist at first
They are produced by the second hidden nest
npcs.gm_notes: Use sparingly before the reveal. Its jaws split into three parts.

npcs.name: Model 1 Seed Clump
npcs.role: Dead Model 1 mass that seeded the second nest
offers: Connection between old nest footage and new nest location
secrets: The clump is made from escaped Model 1s that suicided together
The corporation does not know this happened
npcs.gm_notes: This is a location/hazard more than a character.
